transliteration:
mātrā-sparśās tu kaunteya śītoṣṇa-sukha-duḥkha-dāḥ
āgamāpāyino ’nityās tāṃs titikṣasva bhārata
literal_meaning: Sense-contacts, indeed, O Kaunteya, giving cold-heat-pleasure-pain, coming-going, impermanent—those you must endure, O Bharata.
contextual_meaning: Krishna reframes Arjuna’s despair as transient bodily data, not existential truth; pain is weather, not identity.
psychological_state: Acute stress reaction—hyper-focus on short-term emotional spikes, losing sight of long-term values.
trigger_scenario: You receive critical feedback at work; your nervous system screams 'danger' and you consider quitting, forgetting the bigger career mission.
anchor_text: Let sensations pass like weather; they are not you.
